prompt: Design a Compliance-Grade Event Operations and Accounting Platform to run internal trainings, certification sessions, and community events entirely within an offline facility network. Users include Attendees who create local accounts with a username and password, Event Staff who manage rosters and check-in, Finance Managers who configure accounting and allocations, and System Administrators who control security and audits. In the Vue.js web interface, Attendees browse event sessions, see remaining seat quotas in real time, and register; once a session reaches capacity, the UI automatically offers a waitlist position and clearly shows the cutoff time for promotion. The system blocks duplicate registrations by the same user for the same session and provides immediate feedback explaining the conflict. On event day, Staff use a Check-In screen that displays a rotating 6-digit passcode updated every 60 seconds and enforces a check-in window (for example, 30 minutes before start time through 15 minutes after). Optional anti-proxy rules allow Staff to require a one-time device binding on first check-in per day and warn on concurrent multi-device attempts. A unified notification center within the UI shows templated in-app messages for registration confirmations, waitlist promotions, check-in exceptions, and finance posting results, with user-controlled subscriptions and do-not-disturb hours (default 9:00 PM–7:00 AM). For weak connectivity inside large venues, the UI caches the attendee roster and pending actions locally, supports resumable uploads of attachments, and retries submissions with clear conflict prompts when the same attendee is checked in twice from different devices. The backend uses Spring Boot to expose REST-style APIs that the Vue.js client calls over the local network using HTTPS with request signature verification; all operations must remain functional without any external services, including SMS, email, maps, or third-party identity providers. MySQL persists registrations, waitlists, check-in events, notification templates, send logs, delivery receipts, and security controls, with sensitive fields (such as attendee contact details and device identifiers) encrypted at rest using AES-256 and masked in screens by default. Registration and check-in enforce server-side validation, including quota limits, time-window rules, and a retry queue for notification dispatch with exponential backoff (up to 5 attempts over 10 minutes) and idempotency keys to prevent duplicate sends. A task scheduler and priority queue execute incremental and full crawls of locally defined data sources (for example, importing badge CSV drops from a shared folder), with per-source concurrency caps (default 3 workers), timeouts (30 seconds), circuit breaking after 10 consecutive failures, checkpoint resumes, and end-to-end trace IDs. Finance logic includes configurable accounting periods, a chart of accounts, and cost centers (such as vehicle type or instructor team), with revenue recognition rules (immediate vs. over session dates) and allocation methods (proportional, fixed, or tiered) stored with rule versions and auditable allocation line items. Audit logs capture operator identity, timestamp, request source, and field-level diffs for key actions, searchable by date range and exportable as a locally generated CSV. Security includes least-privilege roles, rate limiting (e.g., 60 requests/minute per user), anti-bot throttles for login, immutable operation logs, nightly local backups with 30-day retention, and configurable watermarking and download restrictions for exported rosters and financial reports.
